title: Device API & wiring
lead: This page documents how IoT devices integrate with Flovers.
Devices can send environmental readings to the backend, check whether a manual watering job has been scheduled from the mobile app, and report completed pump runs back to Flovers.

The generated Arduino/ESP firmware can support both readings and watering control in one sketch. If the linked device was configured with a water pump, the generated code includes pump endpoints and pump-control logic. If the device was configured without a pump, the generated firmware can be simplified to send readings only.

firmwareIntro.title: What the generated firmware does
items: connect to Wi-Fi
synchronize time using NTP
read enabled sensors
send readings to Flovers
check for scheduled manual watering jobs
run the pump when a manual watering job is pending
check backend-controlled automatic watering settings
run automatic watering when soil moisture is below the configured threshold
report watering completion back to the backend
firmwareIntro.defaultTiming: By default, readings are sent once per hour. Manual watering jobs are checked more frequently, for example once per minute, so a watering request created from the mobile app can be picked up without waiting for the next hourly reading cycle.
firmwareIntro.adjustmentNote: Some values should be adjusted for your hardware and preferences, especially Wi-Fi credentials, pin numbers, soil moisture calibration, pump runtime, reading interval, manual job polling interval, and automatic watering cooldown.

ingest.title: Ingest readings
desc: Devices use this endpoint to send sensor readings.
The backend authenticates the request using:
The backend stores readings under the matching device. Readings can include temperature, humidity, light, soil moisture, or any subset of enabled metrics.

pumpNextTask.title: Check next pump task
desc: Devices with pump support use this endpoint to check whether a manual watering job is waiting.
The response also includes the current backend-controlled automatic pump configuration, such as whether automatic watering is enabled and what soil moisture threshold should be used.

pumpComplete.title: Report pump completion
desc: Devices use this endpoint after the pump has run.
This can report either:
items: a scheduled manual watering task, using task_id
or an automatic watering event, using source: "automatic"

feed.title: Read latest feed
desc: This endpoint returns the latest reading data for a device. It is still useful for simple integrations or diagnostics, but the generated pump-enabled firmware uses pump-next-task and pump-complete for watering control.

ingest.title: POST ingest documentation
ingest.desc: Send one reading sample as JSON. The backend authenticates the device and stores the provided metrics.

pumpNextTask.title: POST pump-next-task documentation
desc: This endpoint is used by devices with pump support.
The device asks the backend whether a scheduled manual watering job exists. The backend returns:
items: whether the pump should run now
the task ID if a manual watering task exists
the current automatic pump settings
whether the backend considers the pump and moisture sensor enabled
pumpNextTask.responseNoTask: Example response with no manual watering job
pumpNextTask.responseWithTask: Example response with a manual watering job

pumpComplete.title: POST pump-complete documentation
desc: After the pump runs, the device reports completion.
For manual watering, include the task_id returned by pump-next-task.
For automatic watering, omit task_id and use source: "automatic".

feed.title: GET feed documentation
desc: The feed endpoint returns the latest reading for a device.
It can be used for diagnostics or simple integrations that only need to read the latest stored values.

generatedFirmware.title: Generated Arduino / ESP32 firmware
desc: The app can generate firmware for the selected device.
The generated firmware includes the correct API endpoints, auth secret, device key, device ID, enabled sensors, and pump settings for that device.

If the device is configured with a water pump, the generated code includes both readings and water pump control. It checks manual watering jobs separately from the hourly reading cycle, and automatic watering uses the backend-controlled threshold and toggle.
generatedFirmware.adjustIntro: Before uploading the sketch, adjust the following values for your hardware:
generatedFirmware.codeLabel: Generated Arduino firmware: readings + water pump control

fullCycle.title: Full hourly cycle
fullCycle.desc: Every SEND_INTERVAL_MS, the board runs a full cycle:
items: reads sensors
sends readings to the ingest endpoint
checks for scheduled manual watering
runs manual watering if a task is pending
otherwise checks automatic watering conditions
runs automatic watering if backend settings allow it and soil moisture is below threshold

manualCheck.title: Manual watering check
desc: Every MANUAL_PUMP_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS, the board checks only for scheduled manual watering jobs.
This shorter check:
This allows the app’s “Schedule watering” action to be picked up quickly without forcing sensor readings to be sent every minute.
items: does not send readings
does not run automatic watering
only runs a pending manual watering job if one exists

automatic.title: Automatic watering
desc: Automatic watering is controlled by the backend settings configured in the mobile app.
The board only runs automatic watering when:
items: pump support is enabled
automatic pump launch is enabled
the moisture sensor is enabled
the latest measured soil moisture is below the backend threshold
the automatic watering cooldown has passed
automatic.cooldown: After either manual watering or automatic watering runs, the firmware starts the automatic watering cooldown. This helps avoid a manual watering job and automatic watering happening too close together.

manual.title: Manual watering
desc: Manual watering is scheduled from the mobile app.
Manual watering intentionally ignores:
This means that when the user manually schedules watering, the board treats it as an explicit request to run the pump once.
items: current soil moisture
automatic watering threshold
automatic watering cooldown

hardware.title: Hardware notes
hardware.desc: The generated firmware assumes common ESP32-compatible components:
items: BH1750 / GY-302 light sensor over I2C
BME280 temperature and humidity sensor over I2C
capacitive soil moisture sensor on an analog pin
relay or pump driver connected to a digital output pin
hardware.verify: Verify all wiring before powering the pump.
hardware.relayNote: Some relay modules are active LOW, while others are active HIGH. The generated example uses:
hardware.relayReverse: If your relay works in reverse, you may need to swap the HIGH and LOW values.

calibration.title: Calibration notes
desc: Soil moisture sensors must be calibrated for reliable results.
The generated code uses:
These values are examples only.
Measure your sensor value in dry soil or air, then measure it again in wet soil or water, and update these constants accordingly.

safety.title: Safety notes
desc: The generated firmware is a starting point. Before leaving a pump unattended:
Flovers stores readings and watering events, but device wiring, calibration, pump behavior, and physical installation remain the user’s responsibility.
items: test the pump manually
confirm the relay logic
verify that the pump turns off correctly
use a safe pump runtime
confirm that soil moisture values are realistic
protect electronics from water
use suitable power supply and isolation for the pump
